trial_type.Description: Category of the trial presented to the participant.

Levels.CUE_6_DOT: Presentation of an instructional screen indicating that the upcoming task block will require detecting the appearance of full six-dot Braille cell.
Levels.CUE_REST: Presentation of an instructional screen indicating that the upcoming block is a resting period. Participants are instructed to remain passive and avoid unnecessary movements.
Levels.FIXCROSS: Fixation cross display; no stimuli were presented and the participant remained passive.
Levels.6_DOT_DETECTION: Presentation of tactile stimuli encoded in Braille for target detection.
Levels.RESPONSE: Event marking the participant’s response indicating whether a target Braille cell was detected.

braille_stimulus.Description: Sequence of Braille characters presented to the participant, each encoded in six-dot Braille notation. The symbol '#' denotes a full six-dot Braille cell with all dots raised.

targets.Description: Number of target stimuli (full six-dot Braille cells) presented within the trial.

response_count.Description: Ordinal number of participant's responses in the trial and the number of targets expected in the trial, encoded as n_detected/n_target. Note that the n_detected can be greater than n_target if the participant incorrectly recognised a distractor as the target.
